what_it_does: Documents the uploads/ directory as a place for user-supplied files and states the rules agents must follow when interacting with those files.
observations: Files here are supplied by users via composer/upload affordances
They are not authored by agents and are not part of the committed app
Agents must not depend on specific files existing here
Agents must not edit or delete user uploads
User images should be referenced through a relevant data/state path, not a hardcoded filename
standouts: Explicit boundary between agent-authored code and user-provided content
Prohibition against editing or deleting user uploads
Requirement to reference uploads indirectly via data/state paths
conventions: Separation of user content from committed source
concerns: Does not define the concrete data/state path mechanism or how uploads are discovered
notes: This file sets the governance rules for the uploads/ directory, declaring it off-limits for agent edits and requiring indirect references to user assets.
questions: What is the specific data/state path used to reference an upload?
How does the system discover or register a newly uploaded file?
Are there file type, size, or retention constraints?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches what it does: it describes the uploads/ directory and the agent policy for it.
apparent_themes: Separation of user content from agent-authored code
intention_signals: Establish a safe boundary so agents do not modify or rely on user-provided files, and keep asset references robust against filename changes.
contribution: Contributes a clear policy boundary and a reference-indirection rule that protects user-uploaded assets from agent mutation and hardcoded assumptions.
summary_for_embedding: AGENTS.md in uploads/ defines the directory as a holding area for user-supplied files and instructs agents not to depend on, edit, or delete them, while requiring references through data/state paths instead of hardcoded filenames.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to prevent agents from treating user uploads as committed source or coupling components to specific uploaded filenames.
